extractions.problem: Current prostate cancer diagnostics lack personalized predictive models; need a framework to simulate patient-specific tumour growth using clinical MRI data toward digital twins.
extractions.method: Finite Element Method coupling tumour growth dynamics and oxygen transport, initialized from patient-specific mpMRI biomarkers (ADC cellularity maps, K_Trans vasculature maps), with multi-objective optimization for parameter fitting across patients.
extractions.finding: Framework validated on 4 patients with multiple MRI follow-ups; model predicts prostate and tumour volume growth along with serum PSA levels, representing a preliminary step toward clinical digital twins for prostate cancer.
extractions.dataset: 6 prostate cancer patients under active surveillance (Gleason Score 6), Hospital La Fe Valencia, 2015-2020
extractions.metric: Prostate volume, tumour volume, serum PSA levels

extractions.problem: Personalized medicine requires integrating and processing vast amounts of patient data to find optimal drug treatments.
extractions.method: Proposes constructing high-resolution digital twin models of individual patients, computationally treated with thousands of drugs to identify optimal therapy.
extractions.finding: Clinical implementation of digital twins will require solving technical, medical, ethical, and theoretical challenges comparable in scale to the Human Genome Project, but could lead to greatly improved healthcare.

extractions.problem: PCOS is a common heterogeneous endocrine disorder requiring proper diagnosis and management to prevent metabolic, cardiovascular, and psychiatric complications.
extractions.method: Comprehensive review of epidemiology, diagnostic criteria, pathophysiology, comorbidities, and management strategies for PCOS.
extractions.finding: PCOS prevalence is up to 15-20% with ESHRE/ASRM criteria; insulin resistance affects 50-70% of PCOS women; treatment includes oral contraceptives, anti-androgens, and ovulation induction agents including letrozole and metformin.

extractions.problem: No single RNA-seq analysis pipeline applies to all cases; need a comprehensive review of best practices across all major analysis steps.
extractions.method: Survey and review of RNA-seq analysis steps including experimental design, QC, read alignment, quantification, visualization, differential expression, alternative splicing, functional analysis, gene fusion detection, and eQTL mapping.
extractions.finding: Provides guidelines for each step of RNA-seq analysis with discussion of challenges; highlights that choices of library type, sequencing depth, and replicates must be tailored to the biological question and system under study.

extractions.problem: Health risks of sedentary behaviors are not well known despite widespread insufficient physical activity affecting one-third of the global population aged 15+.
extractions.method: Review of updated evidence on health risks and underlying biological mechanisms of sedentary lifestyle, including effects on lipid metabolism, insulin sensitivity, and vascular function.
extractions.finding: Sedentary behavior increases all-cause mortality, CVD mortality, cancer risk, metabolic disorders, musculoskeletal disorders, depression, and cognitive impairment; 150-300 min/week of moderate physical activity can offset adverse effects.

extractions.problem: Machine learning alone ignores fundamental physics laws leading to ill-posed problems, while multiscale modeling alone fails to efficiently combine large heterogeneous datasets.
extractions.method: Review of integrating machine learning with multiscale modeling across four topical areas: ODEs, PDEs, data-driven approaches, and theory-driven approaches, leveraging multidisciplinary expertise.
extractions.finding: ML and multiscale modeling naturally complement each other to create robust predictive models; integration can provide new insights into disease mechanisms, identify treatment targets, and inform clinical decision making.

extractions.problem: Digital twins for health (DT4H) hold great promise but are still in early stages; need to survey current applications, limitations, and emerging opportunities.
extractions.method: Scoping review of current DT applications in healthcare, consortium research centers, and emerging R&D opportunities.
extractions.finding: DT4H has potential to revolutionize personalized healthcare, predictive interventions, and remote monitoring, but faces challenges in technological innovation, ethics, societal impact, and legal guidance requiring global collaboration.

extractions.problem: Disease rarity, ethical issues regarding placebo arms, time, and cost impede clinical trial conduct for chronic graft-versus-host disease (cGvHD); need virtual control arms.
extractions.method: Constructed a digital twin cohort using Trial Accelerator™ platform from a database of 61M patients (232,909 cohorts); AI algorithm identified patient attributes matching protocol criteria; derived SOC arm for prednisone efficacy assessment.
extractions.finding: Feasible to construct a DT cohort from existing clinical trial data; prednisone ORR at 6 months (52.7%) agrees with literature, demonstrating that a DT SOC arm can potentially replace control arms in clinical trials.
extractions.dataset: cGvHD patients: 2,042 patients in 32 cohorts (flGvHD DT); 438 patients in 8 cohorts (SOC arm)
extractions.result: ORR 52.7% at 6 months, disease onset 7.58 months post-HCT

extractions.problem: Cell identity is governed by complex gene-regulatory networks, but it remains challenging to interpret how transcription factor perturbations alter cell identity from unperturbed data alone.
extractions.method: CellOracle: ML-based GRN inference from single-cell multi-omics (scRNA-seq + scATAC-seq) with in silico TF perturbation simulation using custom network models and vector field visualization.
extractions.finding: Correctly models reported TF perturbation phenotypes in mouse/human hematopoiesis and zebrafish embryogenesis; predicted and experimentally validated a novel noto loss-of-function phenotype and identified lhx1a as an axial mesoderm regulator.
extractions.dataset: Mouse/human hematopoiesis, zebrafish embryogenesis (38,606-cell WT reference atlas at 6/8/10 hpf)
extractions.result: CellOracle with scATAC-seq base GRN outperformed all comparators in AUROC and EPR; robust down to 50 cells

extractions.problem: Accurate identification of B-cell epitopes remains a major challenge for developing epitope-based vaccines, therapeutic antibodies, and diagnostic tools.
extractions.method: Comprehensive review of experimental B-cell epitope mapping methods (X-ray crystallography, NMR, peptide arrays, phage display), epitope databases, and in silico prediction tools based on sequence and structural data.
extractions.finding: Advances in mapping and computational prediction yield molecular insights into antigen-antibody complex formation; however, real epitopes cannot be predicted by ignoring structural effects upon Ag-Ab complex formation, opening space for algorithm improvement.
